text:
And when you look up at me and say
I hope you aren't just saying this to get in my pants
I scramble to respond with
whaaat? nooo ofcoursenotiwouldneverdothat
But what I really mean is:
I would never do that because I'd much rather sit
with you up high on rooftops overlooking
the snow flittering down on people below.
I'd much rather read
quietly in your bedroom
and listen to the wonderful silence
sometimes interrupted
by the turning of a page or
maybe a tiny gasp of shock from the twist.
I'd much rather slide
this poem
over to you while we
sit side by side studying sheepishly
looking at your eyes as they weave left to right over the lines
silently hoping for a squint of joy.
I'd much rather hold
your hand while we walk on the street at night
fingers frostbitten from winter
because we refuse to wear gloves lest
we forget what it's like to feel each others touch.
I'd much rather share
a pair of headphones with you
the wired ones you bought because you were tired of losing the others
I'd much rather listen
to whatever new music you found with those headphones
jam-packed shoulder to shoulder on the subway
with one bud in your ear and the other in mine
and nothing but the wire running down the middle between us
I'd much rather drift asleep on your shoulder
as we watch a movie because
we had been talking about our dreams for hours
and showing my soul makes me drowsy
but I would never do that because
you might think I'm trying to get into your pants
